user.title: Add album slideshow
user.description: Choose a source for the slideshow.

google_shared.title: Google Photos
google_shared.description: Paste a Google Photos shared album share link.

local_folder.title: Local Folder
local_folder.description: Pick a folder path on your Home Assistant filesystem. Use /local/... (maps to /config/www/...) or /media/local/... for NAS-mounted media folders. See the README for details.

media_source.title: Media Source
media_source.description: Paste a Media Source content id pointing at a folder-like node, for example media-source://immich/SERVER_ID|people or media-source://media_source/local/Albums. Browse to the folder in the HA media browser and copy its media-source:// id. Note: Media Source photos have no EXIF, so date, GPS and description features do not apply.

immich.title: Immich
immich.description: Connect directly to your Immich server for full photo metadata (date, location, description). Create an API key in Immich under Account Settings > API Keys.

immich_select.title: Immich source
immich_select.description: Pick a Source, then fill in the matching picker below. Choose Albums or People to combine several at once (each is a searchable list with a Select all option), or pick All photos, Favorites, Random, or Custom search. For Custom search, put an Immich search filter in the Filter field - see the README for examples.

error.invalid_album_url: That does not look like a Google Photos shared album link.
error.invalid_media_source: That does not look like a Media Source id (should start with media-source://).
error.immich_cannot_connect: Could not connect to Immich. Check the URL and API key.
error.immich_no_content: No albums or named people were found on this Immich server.
error.immich_filter_required: Custom search needs a JSON filter.
error.immich_filter_invalid: The filter must be a valid JSON object. See the README for examples.
error.immich_people_required: Pick at least one person for the People source.
error.immich_albums_required: Pick at least one album for the Albums source.

init.title: Local Folder options
init.description: Reverse geocoding sends your photos' EXIF GPS coordinates to the public OpenStreetMap Nominatim service to look up a human-readable place name. Coordinates are rounded to ~100 m before lookup and cached on disk. Turn this off to keep coordinates entirely local; the latitude and longitude attributes still work either way.

data.reverse_geocode: Reverse-geocode EXIF GPS coordinates via OpenStreetMap
